summary: Documented Owlory's long-term app version policy: MARKETING_VERSION / CFBundleShortVersionString is the intentional user-visible release version, while CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION / CFBundleVersion is the per-build identity. The release workflow now covers pre-1.0 and post-1.0 bump intent, ordinary-slice no-bump rules, UTC timestamp build numbers, rollback behavior, tag/changelog expectations, and enterprise traceability across Build Info, GitHub, App Store Connect/TestFlight, MDM rollout notes, and support tickets.
validations_passed: git fetch origin main && git pull --rebase origin main
missing_proof_levels: No app behavior, script behavior, archive behavior, App Store Connect behavior, TestFlight behavior, or MDM rollout behavior was changed or claimed.
MARKETING_VERSION committed-HEAD enforcement remains queued in release-marketing-version-provenance-gate.

contract_status_changes.before: Release workflow documented the scripts and gates but did not state when MARKETING_VERSION should change versus when only CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION should change.
contract_status_changes.after: docs/workflows/release.md now defines MARKETING_VERSION as the intentional user-visible release version and CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION as build identity, with bump rules, rollback rules, and enterprise traceability expectations.

contract_status_changes.before: The app-runtime domain doc still described release mirroring as needing broad automation enforcement even though build-number preflight exists.
contract_status_changes.after: The app-runtime domain doc now names build provenance and archive preflight as implemented, while explicitly preserving the remaining MARKETING_VERSION committed-HEAD enforcement gap.

residual_risks: The policy is documentation-only until the queued MARKETING_VERSION provenance gate and bump-version policy guard slices land.
The policy relies on the current release scripts' timestamp build-number convention; the implementation guard slice should add focused tests around that convention.
recommended_next_reason: The version policy has landed, satisfying the entry condition for the next highest-priority release implementation slice.
